notes: Eval 1 baseline passed all 7 assertions — likely because the general-purpose subagent saw the registered user-stories skill and consulted it even when not explicitly told to. This inflates the baseline and the true with-skill → baseline delta on eval 1 is probably hidden.

Biggest discriminator is eval 2 (anti-pattern detection): with-skill flagged both Misleading Story and Dependency-Locked; baseline caught neither. This is the test case where the skill provides the clearest value.

With-skill runs average ~2x the wall time and ~30% more tokens than the baseline. The cost buys more explicit INVEST annotations, anti-pattern flags, and split rationale — all of which are load-bearing for the skill's purpose.

Eval 0 without-skill wrote Acceptance Criteria even though ACs weren't requested. The skill explicitly says 'Don't add Acceptance Criteria unless the user explicitly asked for them — ACs are out of scope for this skill.' — this rule is doing real work.
